title: 10 best herbal creams for dry lips

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with dry lips because of its unique ability to provide long-lasting hydration. The aloe vera plant contains amino acids, vitamins, and minerals that work together to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips. As the cream is absorbed into the skin, it creates a protective barrier that shields lips from environmental stressors and prevents further drying. This gentle yet effective approach helps to restore healthy lip texture and appearance, leaving them soft, smooth, and supple for hours.
remedy_properties: Emollient property: Herbal aloe creams moisturize and soothe dry, chapped lips by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing dryness and discomfort. This property helps to lock in moisture and reduce flakiness.

Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with dry, cracked lips, promoting a faster recovery from lip irritation.

Antioxidant property: Herbal aloe creams contain antioxidants that neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to dryness and lip lines, helping to prevent premature aging of the lips.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and vitamins, aloe leaves provide soothing and moisturizing properties to help alleviate dry, chapped lips.
Roots: Aloe roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry, irritated lips.
remedy_recipe: Measure and mix 100g of aloe vera gel with 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil.
Combine 50g of shea butter with 15g of sweet almond oil in a separate mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture containing beeswax, coconut oil, and aloe vera gel for 5 minutes to melt the beeswax.
Gradually add the shea butter and sweet almond oil mixture to the melted beeswax while stirring constantly for 2 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree creams helps with dry lips because they provide intense moisturizing properties that deeply nourish and hydrate the skin. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory agents present in tea tree oil help to soothe and calm chapped, flaky lips, while also reducing redness and irritation. Additionally, the natural emollients and humectants in the cream create a protective barrier on the surface of the skin, locking in moisture and preventing further dryness.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial Properties: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dry, chapped lips, promoting healthy lip skin.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in tea tree oil reduce redness and swelling associated with dry, irritated lips, providing soothing relief and comfort.
Emollient Properties: Herbal extracts like aloe vera and chamomile in the cream help lock in moisture, hydrate, and soften dry, chapped lip skin, reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain high levels of antioxidants and essential oils that help to soothe and moisturize dry, chapped lips.
Roots: The roots of the tea tree plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality melaleuca alternifolia essential oil 10ml, beeswax pellets 30g, coconut oil 50ml and vitamin e oil 2ml.
Melt the beeswax pellets in a double boiler set at medium heat for about 15 minutes until fully liquefied.
Add the melaleuca alternifolia essential oil and stir well to combine with the melted beeswax.
Slowly pour in the coconut oil while continuously whisking to ensure a smooth and consistent texture develops.
Pour the mixture into lip balm tubes or containers and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for about 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with dry lips because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb contains apigenin, an antioxidant that calms irritated skin and reduces redness, while also providing moisturizing benefits. When applied topically to chapped or dry lips, chamomile cream can help lock in moisture, reduce flakiness, and promote a smooth, soft texture. This natural remedy can provide long-lasting relief from dry, cracked lips, leaving them feeling comfortable and looking healthy.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness and swelling associated with dry, chapped lips, providing instant relief from irritation.
Antimicrobial property: Chamomile's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate dry, cracked lips, promoting a healthy environment for lip healing.

Soothing and calming property: The apigenin and luteolin present in chamomile have a sedative effect on the skin, helping to calm and soothe dry, irritated lips, reducing discomfort and promoting moisturization.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The delicate petals of the chamomile flower contain soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds that calm dry, chapped lips.
Roots: The rich antioxidants and flavonoids in chamomile roots help to nourish and moisturize dry lips, promoting healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Measure and combine one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and two teaspoons of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler setup over low heat for 15-20 minutes, allowing the chamomile to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, squeezing gently to extract as much oil as possible from the solids.
Add one teaspoon of beeswax and two teaspoons of coconut cream to the infused oil and heat the mixture in the double boiler setup for an additional 5-7 minutes until melted.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before transferring the cream to a lip balm container, allowing it to solidify completely at room temperature within 30-60 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with dry lips because of its soothing and nourishing properties. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds found in lavender calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. As a natural emollient, it moisturizes the lips, locking in moisture to combat dryness. Additionally, lavender's antibacterial properties help to prevent infection and promote healthy healing. This unique combination of benefits makes English lavender creams an effective remedy for relieving dry, chapped lips and promoting soft, supple lips.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lavender's antibacterial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can cause inflammation and dryness on the skin, soothing and healing cracked lips.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with dry, chapped lips, promoting a more comfortable and hydrated appearance.
Moisturizing properties: Lavender's moisturizing properties help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked lips by reducing water loss and creating a protective barrier on the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, making them effective at soothing and moisturizing dry lips.
Seeds: Containing essential oils that provide anti-inflammatory properties, helping to calm and protect chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers and dry them for at least 48 hours.
Combine 30g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to ensure even infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding solids and retaining liquid.
Mix 100ml of the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating until smooth and melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with dry lips because of its unique properties. The herb marshmallow, also known as Althaea officinalis, contains mucilages that provide long-lasting hydration to parched lips. These mucilages form a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and soothing dryness. Additionally, the cream's natural emollients and antioxidants work together to soften and calm irritated lips, leaving them feeling smooth, supple, and protected. This natural remedy provides instant relief for dry, chapped, or cracked lips.
remedy_properties: Emollient and Humectant Properties: Marshmallow creams contain emollients like glycosides, which fill in the gaps between skin cells to lock in moisture and soften dry, chapped lips. This helps to hydrate and soothe dryness.

Anti-Inflammatory and Anti-Irritant Properties: The saponins present in marshmallows have anti-inflammatory and anti-irritant properties, which help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry, chapped lips, while also calming irritation and inflammation.

Mucilages and Gums: Marshmallow creams contain mucilages like galactomannans, which form a protective film on the skin's surface, providing a barrier against environmental stressors and preventing moisture loss, further helping to soothe and hydrate dry lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant are rich in mucilage, a natural thickening agent that helps to soothe and moisturize dry lips.
Buds: Marshmallow buds contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to calm and protect dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality marshmallow root (althaea officinalis) dried flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Combine the powdered marshmallow root with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a small saucepan, heating the mixture over low heat for 15-20 minutes.
Stir in vitamin e oil and lavender essential oil to the mixture, then remove it from heat and let it cool down to around 120°f.
Whip heavy cream until it becomes stiff peaks, then gently fold it into the cooled marshmallow root mixture using a spatula for about 5-7 minutes.
Pour the prepared cream into sterilized containers, label them, and store them in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks or in the freezer for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with dry lips because of its unique ability to provide intense hydration and soothing relief. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling, while its antioxidant content protects the lips from environmental stressors that can exacerbate dryness. As a natural emollient, pot marigold cream fills in cracks and crevices, leaving lips feeling smooth and soft. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties prevent bacterial growth, helping to combat infections and promote healthy lip function.
remedy_properties: Calendula Oil's Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Calendula oil, extracted from pot marigold flowers, has anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with dry lips.

Antioxidant and Antimicrobial Activity: The antioxidants and antimicrobial agents present in pot marigold creams help protect the lips from environmental stressors and prevent infections, promoting healthy lip tissue and preventing further dryness.

Emollient and Moisturizing Properties: Pot marigold creams contain emollients that lock in moisture, softening and hydrating dry, chapped lips, while also helping to repair and restore the skin's natural barrier function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe and calm dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: Containing a high concentration of saponins, which provide moisturizing and emollient properties to lip balms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula flowers and dry them according to standard herbal medicine protocols for 2 weeks.
Grind dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle within 15 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of powdered calendula with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler under low heat for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and add 5 grams of beeswax, stirring until melted and fully incorporated in 10 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in a cool dark place at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Geranium creams helps with dry lips because of its unique ability to moisturize and soothe chapped, cracked skin. The herbal extract in geranium cream provides intense hydration, penetrating deep into the lip tissue to repair and restore its natural barrier function. Additionally, geranium's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and swelling, promoting a healthy and comfortable lip environment. As a result, geranium creams provide long-lasting relief from dry, chapped lips, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and supple.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Geranium oil contains antioxidants that help protect the lips from environmental stressors, such as UV radiation and pollution, which can contribute to dryness and chapping.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in geranium oil, such as citronellol and geraniol, help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry, cracked lips, promoting a more comfortable and hydrated appearance.

Moisturizing Properties: Geranium cream's moisturizing properties, which come from the fatty acid content of the herb, help lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips, leaving them soft and supple.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, geranium roots help soothe and calm dry, chapped lips.
Barks: Geranium barks contain flavonoids and tannins that provide a protective barrier for dry lips while also reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pelargonium graveolens leaves and chop them into small pieces weighing approximately 100 grams.
Combine the chopped leaves with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a dark place for four to six weeks, allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 80 milliliters of the infused oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler until it reaches a temperature of 180°f for approximately one hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cacao creams helps with dry lips because of their rich antioxidant properties, which deeply nourish and moisturize the lips. The fatty acid content in cacao creams creates a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing trans-epidermal water loss. Additionally, the emollients present in these creams help to fill in the gaps between skin cells, providing long-lasting hydration and softening of dry, chapped lips. As a result, cacao creams provide natural relief from dryness, leaving lips feeling smooth, supple, and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Cacao cream's antioxidants help to protect the lip skin from environmental stressors and damage, promoting healthy cell growth and reducing inflammation, which can exacerbate dryness.

Emollient and Moisturizing Properties: The fatty acids and glycerides in cacao cream create a barrier on the lip surface, locking in moisture and protecting against water loss, soothing and hydrating dry, chapped lips.
Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Cacao's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness and swelling associated with dry, irritated lips, promoting comfort and relief.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, cacao seeds are often used to create nourishing lip balms that soothe and moisturize dry lips.
Buds: The tender buds of the cacao plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and irritation associated with dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried theobroma cacao leaves, 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, and 1 tablespoon of beeswax.
Combine the ingredients in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over simmering water.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil mixture, stirring occasionally, for approximately 10-15 minutes.
Add 20 drops of vitamin e essential oil and 10 drops of lavender essential oil to the melted mixture, then stir well to combine.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature, whipping gently with a spatula until it thickens and emulsifies within about 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet briar creams helps with dry lips because they contain natural emollients that deeply moisturize and nourish the skin. The herbs in sweet briar, such as chamomile and calendula, have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated lips. Additionally, sweet briar creams create a protective barrier on the surface of the lip skin, locking in moisture and preventing dryness and chapping. As a result, lips become soft, smooth, and supple, leaving them looking healthy and radiant.
remedy_properties: Emollient properties: Sweet briar cream's emollient properties help to soften, moisturize, and soothe dry, chapped lips by filling in gaps between skin cells and locking in moisture.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in sweet briar cream help to protect the lips from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and cracking.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sweet briar's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry, chapped lips, promoting a more comfortable and healthy-looking lip appearance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of glycosides and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and protect dry, chapped lips.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes are rich in bioactive compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which possess emollient and moisturizing properties, making them ideal for hydrating and softening dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rubiginosa petals and dried rosa centifolia flowers in equal amounts measuring about one tablespoon each.
Combine the harvested petals and flowers with two tablespoons of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 4-6 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container and discard the solids after 10 minutes.
Mix one teaspoon of beeswax and half-teaspoon of vitamin e oil with the infused oil in a double boiler until the mixture melts completely in about 5-7 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into small tin containers or lip balm tubes and allow it to set at room temperature for about 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with dry lips because of its natural moisturizing properties. The antiseptic and antibacterial compounds present in thyme, such as thymol, help to soothe and calm irritated lips, while also reducing inflammation. Additionally, the cream's emollient base locks in moisture, providing long-lasting hydration and comfort to dry, chapped lips. As a result, herbal thyme creams can effectively relieve dry, cracked, or flaky lips, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and supple.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help to combat fungal and bacterial infections that can cause dry, cracked lips, promoting healthy lip function.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry, chapped lips, providing relief and soothing the skin.
Emollient properties: Thyme's emollients help to moisturize and soften dry lips, filling in cracks and crevices while protecting the skin from further drying and irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help to soothe and calm dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: Packed with vitamins and minerals, leaves provide essential moisture and nourishment to dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers then chop them finely.
Combine 20g of dried herbs with 100ml of sweet almond oil in a heatproof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes then strain through cheesecloth.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 25g of coconut oil and add to the herbal infusion.
Stir until everything melts and emulsifies then pour into sterilized tubes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with dry lips because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The cream's hydrating ingredients, such as glycerin and panthenol, work to lock in moisture and provide long-lasting relief from chapped and cracked lips. Additionally, sage's antibacterial properties help to prevent infection and promote healing, allowing dry and damaged lips to recover quickly and naturally. This combination of benefits makes herbal sage creams an effective solution for dry and chapped lips.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which helps to soothe and calm dry, irritated skin on the lips, reducing redness and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidant properties help to protect the lips from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy lip cell growth and reducing the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
Moisturizing properties: Sage contains natural oils and waxes that help to lock in moisture and soothe dryness on the lips, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming dry, chapped lips.
Barks: The barks of the sage plant have antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infection and promote healing of dry, cracked lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves at dawn or evening when potency is highest.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Blend dried salvia officinalis leaves with beeswax and coconut oil in a ratio of 1:3:4.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring until the beeswax melts completely.
Strain the hot mixture through cheesecloth into sterilized glass containers for immediate use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with dry lips because of their soothing and moisturizing properties. The peppermint extract in these creams provides a cooling sensation that calms irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry lips. Additionally, the cream's emollient ingredients, such as coconut oil and shea butter, work to lock in moisture, leaving lips feeling soft, smooth, and supple. This dual-action approach helps to not only relieve dryness but also promote healthy lip development.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing and soothing: Peppermint's emollient properties help to lock in moisture, calm irritation, and reduce dryness on the lips, providing relief from discomfort and chapping.

Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint's menthol content has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and redness associated with dry, chapped lips, promoting a sense of comfort and ease.

Antioxidant and protectant: Peppermint's antioxidant compounds help protect the lips from environmental stressors and free radicals, which can contribute to dryness and damage, while also promoting healthy skin cell regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of peppermint are rich in menthol, which helps to moisturize and soothe dry, chapped lips.
Leaves: Peppermint leaves contain high amounts of vitamins A and E, which work together to nourish and protect dry, cracked lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves and rinse them thoroughly under running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the peppermint leaves into small pieces and combine them with 100g of beeswax in a double boiler set over low heat for 5 minutes.
Add 20ml of sweet almond oil and 10ml of coconut oil to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated taking approximately 3 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 40°c before whipping with an electric mixer for 5 minutes until it thickens slightly.
Pour the cream into a sterilized container and store it in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with dry lips because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, work to calm irritated skin and lock in moisture. This helps to reduce flakiness and discomfort associated with dry lips, leaving them feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated. Additionally, lemon balm's natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help to prevent infection and soothe redness, promoting healthy-looking lips.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm cream's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm dry, chapped lips by reducing inflammation and protecting them from further damage caused by environmental stressors.

Moisturizing and emollient properties: The moisturizing and emollient properties of lemon balm cream help to lock in moisture and provide long-lasting hydration to dry, cracked lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and supple.

Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Lemon balm's antiviral and antibacterial properties help combat infections that can cause dry, chapped lips by preventing the growth of harmful microorganisms and promoting a healthy lip environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lemon balm leaves have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and protect dry lips.
Buds: The small, tightly closed buds on the lemon balm plant contain high concentrations of antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help lock in moisture and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and chop them finely to release their essential oils.
Combine chopped melissa officinalis with sweet almond oil in a 10% herb-to-oil ratio by weight.
Heat the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes under gentle stirring to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard solids.
Mix the strained oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a 20% beeswax-to-oil ratio by weight, whipping until fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with dry lips because they provide intense moisturizing properties that deeply hydrate the lip tissue. The natural oils and extracts present in ginger, such as glycerin and vitamin E, work together to lock in moisture and soothe dryness, leaving lips feeling soft, supple, and comfortable. Additionally, ginger's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce irritation and redness, promoting healthy-looking lips that are less prone to flakiness and cracking.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal ginger creams contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress on the skin, which can cause lip dryness and chapping.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dry, chapped lips.
Moisturizing properties: Herbal ginger creams often contain natural emollients like coconut oil, shea butter, or beeswax that help lock in moisture and soothe dry, cracked skin on the lips.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rhizomes help soothe and moisturize dry lips.
Roots: Containing natural emollients and humectants, roots provide intense hydration and nourishment to dry lips.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger rhizomes and peel off the skin to reveal the inner layer.
Grind 20 grams of peeled ginger into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar pestle.
Combine 30 grams of beeswax pellets with 40 grams of sweet almond oil and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 5 grams of the ginger powder to the melted wax mixture and stir until well combined, taking 10 minutes to prepare.
Pour the mixture into small containers, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature over 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with dry lips because of their unique blend of natural ingredients, including the herb Echinacea purpurea. This herb contains anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe and calm dry, chapped lips, while its moisturizing properties help lock in moisture and hydrate the skin. Additionally, the cream's emollient-rich formula creates a barrier on the surface of the lips, shielding them from harsh environmental factors and promoting a smoother, softer appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds could help soothe and protect dry, irritated lips by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Hydrating and moisturizing effects: Some species of echinacea have been shown to possess humectant properties, which could contribute to the cream's ability to retain moisture and provide long-lasting hydration to dry lips.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial properties: Echinacea has been traditionally used to treat wounds and infections. In a lip care context, these properties might help accelerate the healing process of chapped or cracked lips by promoting tissue repair and reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm dry, chapped lips.
Seeds: Containing fatty acids and other nutrients, seeds provide moisturizing and nourishing properties to relieve dry lip symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm, well-ventilated area for at least three weeks.
Grind 20 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered echinacea with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with dry lips because of its unique ability to soothe and moisturize the skin. The herb licorice root contains potent anti-inflammatory compounds that calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, the cream's rich emollient properties provide long-lasting hydration, filling in cracks and crevices to leave lips feeling soft, supple, and protected from further dryness. This natural remedy offers a gentle, non-irritating solution for parched, chapped lips, leaving them looking healthy and luscious.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins, a type of triterpenoid, help to moisturize and soothe dry, chapped lips by forming a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing inflammation.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a compound found in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with dry, chapped lips, promoting a more comfortable and healthy-looking lip appearance.

Antioxidants: The antioxidant compounds present in herbal licorice creams help to protect the lips from environmental stressors like UV rays, pollution, and cold weather, which can cause damage and dryness, thereby supporting overall lip health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, which provides moisturizing and soothing properties that help to alleviate dryness and irritation on the lips.

Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain compounds like liquiritin and isoliquiritin that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them effective for calming and protecting dry, chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in sweet almond oil for 2 hours at room temperature.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids retaining 250ml of the infused oil.
Mix 10g of beeswax pellets and 20g of shea butter with the infused oil and heat mixture until melted and combined.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 30 minutes then whip in 5 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers filling to the brim and store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tamarind creams helps with dry lips because they provide long-lasting hydration and nourishment to the skin. The herbal extracts in these creams, such as glycerin and panthenol, help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips. Additionally, tamarind's natural antibacterial properties help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection. As a result, tamarind creams offer effective relief from dry, cracked lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and comfortable for hours.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Tamarind's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to lip cells, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy lip tissue.

Humectant properties: The mucilage present in tamarind helps retain moisture on the skin's surface, locking it in and providing long-lasting hydration to dry lips, while also soothing irritation and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Tamarind's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with dry, chapped, or irritated lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, tamarind roots help soothe and moisturize dry lips.
Barks: Tannins present in tamarind bark provide a natural astringent property, helping to reduce lip moisture loss and lock in hydration.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250g of dried tamarindus indica seeds, 50ml of coconut oil, 20ml of sweet almond oil, and 10ml of beeswax.
Grind the dried tamarindus indica seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground tamarindus indica powder with the coconut oil, sweet almond oil, and beeswax in a heat-resistant bowl until well combined.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 65°c.
Remove from heat, let cool to room temperature, and whip the mixture with an electric mixer or spatula for 2-3 minutes until it thickens into a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Shea butter tree creams helps with dry lips because of their rich moisturizing properties. The cream's key ingredient, shea butter, is derived from the nut of the African shea tree and is naturally packed with vitamins A and E, as well as fatty acids. These nutrients deeply nourish and hydrate dry lips, providing long-lasting relief and comfort. As a result, dry, chapped lips are soothed and rejuvenated, leaving them smooth and healthy-looking.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing and hydrating: Shea butter tree creams help to lock in moisture and soothe dry, chapped lips, providing long-lasting hydration.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties in these creams help to protect lips from damage caused by environmental stressors and inflammation, reducing dryness and irritation.

Protective and barrier-forming: The fatty acids and lipids in shea butter tree creams create a protective barrier on the skin's surface, preventing moisture loss and shielding lips from harsh conditions.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in vitamins A and E, which help to moisturize and nourish dry, chapped lips.
Roots: Contain healing compounds that soothe and calm dry, irritated skin, making them ideal for dry lips.
Buds: Similar to the sap of the tree, buds are rich in antioxidants that help to hydrate and protect dry skin.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 30g of dried and powdered vitellaria paradoxa bark and set aside for later use.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 20g of coconut oil in a heat-proof container.
Add 30g of the powdered vitellaria paradoxa bark to the heat-proof container containing the beeswax and coconut oil mixture.
Stir the mixture constantly for 10 minutes until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth and consistent.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and harden completely before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for dry lips are natural moisturizing products that contain plant-based ingredients, such as aloe vera, chamomile, and beeswax, to provide long-lasting relief from chapped and cracked lips. These creams help to lock in moisture, soothe irritation, and promote healing, making them an effective solution for dry, flaky, or even painful lips. For example, products like Burt's Bees Lip Balm with beeswax and coconut oil can help to soften and hydrate lips, while brands like Laneige's Lip Sleeping Mask with green tea extract can provide overnight nourishment. As a result, individuals can enjoy softer, smoother lips and improved overall oral health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry lips with herbal creams is to apply a rich balm containing soothing herbs like aloe vera, chamomile, or calendula. These herbs have natural anti-inflammatory properties that calm dry, cracked lips. Look for creams infused with beeswax, coconut oil, and vitamin E to lock in moisture and promote healing. Apply the cream regularly throughout the day to keep lips soft, supple, and protected from harsh environmental conditions.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry lips that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, acne, and dermatitis. Herbal creams can provide relief from itchiness, inflammation, and redness associated with these conditions. Ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of dryness and discomfort. These herbal creams are gentle on the skin and can be used as an alternative to harsh chemical-based treatments.
